options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: starting_world
options.description: This sets which world you start in. Other worlds can be accessed by receiving a Gate respective to that world.

options.name: starting_lives
options.description: This sets the amount of lives Yoshi will have upon loading the game.

options.name: goal
options.description: This sets the goal. Bowser goal requires defeating Bowser at the end of 6-8, while Luigi Hunt requires collecting all required Luigi Pieces.

options.name: luigi_pieces_required
options.description: This will set how many Luigi Pieces are required to trigger a victory.

options.name: luigi_pieces_in_pool
options.description:
This will set how many Luigi Pieces are in the item pool.
If the number in the pool is lower than the number required,
the amount in the pool will be randomized, with the minimum being the amount required.

options.name: extras_enabled
options.description: If enabled, the more difficult Extra stages will be added into logic. Otherwise, they will be inaccessible.

options.name: minigame_checks
options.description:
This will set minigame victories to give Archipelago checks.
This will not randomize minigames amongst themselves, and is compatible with item logic.
Bonus games will be expected to be cleared from the Overworld, not the end of levels.
Additionally, 1-Up bonus games will accept any profit as a victory.

options.name: split_extras
options.description: If enabled, Extra stages will be unlocked individually. Otherwise, there will be a single 'Extra Panels' item that unlocks all of them.

options.name: split_bonus
options.description: If enabled, Bonus Games will be unlocked individually. Otherwise, there will be a single 'Bonus Panels' item that unlocks all of them.

options.name: hidden_object_visibility
options.description:
This will determine the default visibility of objects revealed by the Magnifying Glass.
Strict Logic will expect the Secret Lens or a Magnifying Glass to interact with hidden clouds containing stars if they are not set to visible by default.

options.name: add_secretlens
options.description:
If enabled, the Secret Lens will be added to the item pool.
The Secret Lens will act as a permanent Magnifying Glass.

options.name: shuffle_midrings
options.description: If enabled, Middle Rings will be added to the item pool.

options.name: stage_logic
options.description:
This determines what logic mode the stages will use.
Strict: Best for casual players or those new to playing Yoshi's Island in AP. Level requirements won't expect anything too difficult of the player.
Loose: Recommended for veterans of the original game. Won't expect anything too difficult, but may expect unusual platforming or egg throws.
Expert: Logic may expect advanced knowledge or memorization of level layouts, as well as jumps the player may only have one chance to make without restarting.

options.name: item_logic
options.description:
This will enable logic to expect consumables to be used from the inventory in place of some major items.
Logic will expect you to have access to an Overworld bonus game, or a bandit game to get the necessary items.
Logic will NOT expect grinding end-of-level bonus games, or any inventory consumables received from checks.
Casual logic will only expect consumables from Overworld games; Loose and Expert may expect them from bandit games.

options.name: disable_autoscroll
options.description: If enabled, will disable autoscrolling during levels, except during levels which cannot function otherwise.

options.name: softlock_prevention
options.description: If enabled, hold R + X to warp to the last used Middle Ring, or the start of the level if none have been activated.

options.name: castle_open_condition
options.description:
This sets how many bosses need to be defeated to access 6-8.
You can check this in-game by pressing SELECT while in any level.

options.name: castle_clear_condition
options.description:
This sets how many bosses need to be defeated to access the boss of 6-8.
You can check this in-game by pressing SELECT while in any level.

options.name: bowser_door_mode
options.description:
This will set which route you take through 6-8.
Manual: You go through the door that you hit with an egg, as normal.
Doors: Route will be forced to be the door chosen here, regardless of which door you hit.
Gauntlet: You will be forced to go through all 4 routes in order before the final hallway.

options.name: level_shuffle
options.description:
Disabled: All levels will appear in their normal location.
Bosses Guaranteed: All worlds will have a boss on -4 and -8.
Full: Worlds may have more than 2 or no bosses in them.
Regardless of the setting, 6-8 and Extra stages are not shuffled.

options.name: boss_shuffle
options.description: This whill shuffle which boss each boss door will lead to. Each boss can only appear once, and Baby Bowser is left alone.

options.name: yoshi_colors
options.description:
Sets the Yoshi color for each level.
Normal will use the vanilla colors.
Random order will generate a random order of colors that will be used in each level. The stage 1 color will be used for Extra stages, and 6-8.
Random color will generate a random color for each stage.
Singularity will use a single color defined under 'Singularity Yoshi Color' for use in all stages.

options.name: yoshi_singularity_color
options.description: Sets which color Yoshi will be if Yoshi Colors is set to singularity.

options.name: baby_mario_sound
options.description: Change the sound that Baby Mario makes when not on Yoshi.

options.name: traps_enabled
options.description:
Will place traps into the item pool.
Traps have a variety of negative effects, and will only replace filler items.

options.name: trap_percent
options.description: Percentage of the item pool that becomes replaced with traps.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
